1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: require 'zip'
4: 
5: class BackupService < BaseService
6:   include Payloadable
7:   include ContextHelper
8: 
9:   CHUNK_SIZE = 1.megabyte
10:   PLACEHOLDER = '!PLACEHOLDER!'
11: 
12:   STREAM_ACTOR = 'actor.json'
13:   STREAM_BOOKMARKS = 'bookmarks.json'
14:   STREAM_LIKES = 'likes.json'
15:   STREAM_OUTBOX = 'outbox.json'
16: 
17:   attr_reader :account, :backup
18: 
19:   def call(backup)
20:     @backup  = backup
21:     @account = backup.user.account
22: 
23:     build_archive!
24:   end
25: 
26:   private
27: 
28:   def build_outbox_json!(file)
29:     skeleton = serialize(collection_presenter(STREAM_OUTBOX, size: account.statuses.count), ActivityPub::CollectionSerializer)
30:     skeleton[:@context] = full_context
31:     skeleton[:orderedItems] = [PLACEHOLDER]
32:     skeleton = skeleton.to_json
33:     prepend, append = skeleton.split(PLACEHOLDER.to_json)
34: 
35:     file.write(prepend)
36: 
37:     account.statuses.with_includes.reorder(nil).find_in_batches.with_index do |statuses, batch|
38:       file.write(',') unless batch.zero?
39: 
40:       file.write(statuses.map do |status|
41:         serializer = status.reblog? ? ActivityPub::AnnounceNoteSerializer : ActivityPub::CreateNoteSerializer
42:         item = serialize_payload(status, serializer)
43:         item.delete(:@context)
44: 
45:         unless item[:type] == 'Announce' || item[:object][:attachment].blank?
46:           item[:object][:attachment].each do |attachment|
47:             attachment[:url] = Addressable::URI.parse(attachment[:url]).path.delete_prefix('/system/')
48:           end
49:         end
50: 
51:         item.to_json
52:       end.join(','))
53: 
54:       GC.start
55:     end
56: 
57:     file.write(append)
58:   end
59: 
60:   def build_archive!
61:     tmp_file = Tempfile.new(%w(archive .zip))
62: 
63:     build_zip_file(tmp_file)
64: 
65:     @backup.dump = ActionDispatch::Http::UploadedFile.new(tempfile: tmp_file, filename: archive_filename)
66:     @backup.processed = true
67:     @backup.save!
68:   ensure
69:     tmp_file.close
70:     tmp_file.unlink
71:   end
72: 
73:   def build_zip_file(file)
74:     Zip::File.open(file, create: true) do |zip|
75:       dump_outbox!(zip)
76:       dump_media_attachments!(zip)
77:       dump_likes!(zip)
78:       dump_bookmarks!(zip)
79:       dump_actor!(zip)
80:     end
81:   end
82: 
83:   def archive_filename
84:     "#{archive_id}.zip"
85:   end
86: 
87:   def archive_id
88:     [:archive, Time.current.to_fs(:number), SecureRandom.hex(16)].join('-')
89:   end
90: 
91:   def dump_media_attachments!(zipfile)
92:     MediaAttachment.attached.where(account: account).find_in_batches do |media_attachments|
93:       media_attachments.each do |m|
94:         path = m.file&.path
95:         next unless path
96: 
97:         path = path.gsub(%r{\A.*/system/}, '')
98:         path = path.gsub(%r{\A/+}, '')
99:         download_to_zip(zipfile, m.file, path)
100:       end
101: 
102:       GC.start
103:     end
104:   end
105: 
106:   def dump_outbox!(zipfile)
107:     zipfile.get_output_stream(STREAM_OUTBOX) do |io|
108:       build_outbox_json!(io)
109:     end
110:   end
111: 
112:   def dump_actor!(zipfile)
113:     actor = serialize(account, ActivityPub::ActorSerializer)
114: 
115:     actor[:icon][:url]  = "avatar#{File.extname(actor[:icon][:url])}"  if actor[:icon]
116:     actor[:image][:url] = "header#{File.extname(actor[:image][:url])}" if actor[:image]
117:     actor[:outbox]      = STREAM_OUTBOX
118:     actor[:likes]       = STREAM_LIKES
119:     actor[:bookmarks]   = STREAM_BOOKMARKS
120: 
121:     download_to_zip(zipfile, account.avatar, "avatar#{File.extname(account.avatar.path)}") if account.avatar.exists?
122:     download_to_zip(zipfile, account.header, "header#{File.extname(account.header.path)}") if account.header.exists?
123: 
124:     zipfile.get_output_stream(STREAM_ACTOR) do |io|
125:       io.write(actor.to_json)
126:     end
127:   end
128: 
129:   def dump_likes!(zipfile)
130:     skeleton = serialize(collection_presenter(STREAM_LIKES), ActivityPub::CollectionSerializer)
131: 
132:     skeleton.delete(:totalItems)
133:     skeleton[:orderedItems] = [PLACEHOLDER]
134:     skeleton = skeleton.to_json
135:     prepend, append = skeleton.split(PLACEHOLDER.to_json)
136: 
137:     zipfile.get_output_stream(STREAM_LIKES) do |io|
138:       io.write(prepend)
139: 
140:       favourite_statuses.find_in_batches.with_index do |statuses, batch|
141:         io.write(',') unless batch.zero?
142: 
143:         io.write(statuses.map do |status|
144:           ActivityPub::TagManager.instance.uri_for(status).to_json
145:         end.join(','))
146: 
147:         GC.start
148:       end
149: 
150:       io.write(append)
151:     end
152:   end
153: 
154:   def favourite_statuses
155:     Status.reorder(nil).joins(:favourites).includes(:account).merge(account.favourites)
156:   end
157: 
158:   def dump_bookmarks!(zipfile)
159:     skeleton = serialize(collection_presenter(STREAM_BOOKMARKS), ActivityPub::CollectionSerializer)
160:     skeleton.delete(:totalItems)
161:     skeleton[:orderedItems] = [PLACEHOLDER]
162:     skeleton = skeleton.to_json
163:     prepend, append = skeleton.split(PLACEHOLDER.to_json)
164: 
165:     zipfile.get_output_stream(STREAM_BOOKMARKS) do |io|
166:       io.write(prepend)
167: 
168:       bookmark_statuses.find_in_batches.with_index do |statuses, batch|
169:         io.write(',') unless batch.zero?
170: 
171:         io.write(statuses.map do |status|
172:           ActivityPub::TagManager.instance.uri_for(status).to_json
173:         end.join(','))
174: 
175:         GC.start
176:       end
177: 
178:       io.write(append)
179:     end
180:   end
181: 
182:   def bookmark_statuses
183:     Status.reorder(nil).joins(:bookmarks).includes(:account).merge(account.bookmarks)
184:   end
185: 
186:   def collection_presenter(id, size: 0)
187:     ActivityPub::CollectionPresenter.new(
188:       id:,
189:       items: [],
190:       size:,
191:       type: :ordered
192:     )
193:   end
194: 
195:   def serialize(object, serializer)
196:     ActiveModelSerializers::SerializableResource.new(
197:       object,
198:       serializer: serializer,
199:       adapter: ActivityPub::Adapter
200:     ).as_json
201:   end
202: 
203:   def download_to_zip(zipfile, attachment, filename)
204:     adapter = Paperclip.io_adapters.for(attachment)
205: 
206:     zipfile.get_output_stream(filename) do |io|
207:       while (buffer = adapter.read(CHUNK_SIZE))
208:         io.write(buffer)
209:       end
210:     end
211:   rescue Errno::ENOENT, Seahorse::Client::NetworkingError => e
212:     Rails.logger.warn "Could not backup file #{filename}: #{e}"
213:   end
214: end

18:     return reject_payload! if unsupported_object_type? || non_matching_uri_hosts?(@account.uri, object_uri) || tombstone_exists? || !related_to_local_activity?
19: 
20:     with_redis_lock("create:#{object_uri}") do
21:       Status.uncached do
22:         return if delete_arrived_first?(object_uri) || poll_vote?
23: 
24:         @status = find_existing_status
25:       end
26: 
27:       if @status.nil?
28:         process_status
29:       elsif @options[:delivered_to_account_id].present?
30:         postprocess_audience_and_deliver
31:       end
32:     end
33: 
34:     @status
35:   end
36: 
37:   def audience_to
38:     as_array(@object['to'] || @json['to']).map { |x| value_or_id(x) }
39:   end
40: 
41:   def audience_cc
42:     as_array(@object['cc'] || @json['cc']).map { |x| value_or_id(x) }
43:   end
44: 
45:   def process_status
46:     @tags                 = []
47:     @mentions             = []
48:     @tagged_objects       = []
49:     @unresolved_mentions  = []
50:     @unresolved_collections = []
51:     @silenced_account_ids = []

53:     @quote                = nil
54:     @quote_uri            = nil

56: 
57:     process_status_params
58:     process_tags
59:     process_quote
60:     process_audience

62:     ApplicationRecord.transaction do
63:       @status = Status.create!(@params.merge(quote: @quote))
64:       attach_tags(@status)

66:       attach_mentions(@status)
67:       attach_counts(@status)

69: 
70:     resolve_thread(@status)
71:     resolve_unresolved_mentions(@status)
72:     resolve_unresolved_collections(@status)
73:     fetch_replies(@status)
74:     fetch_and_verify_quote
75:     distribute
76:     forward_for_reply

79:   def distribute
80:     # Spread out crawling randomly to avoid DDoSing the link
81:     LinkCrawlWorker.perform_in(rand(DISTRIBUTE_DELAY), @status.id)
82: 
83:     # Distribute into home and list feeds and notify mentioned accounts
84:     ::DistributionWorker.perform_async(@status.id, { 'silenced_account_ids' => @silenced_account_ids }) if @options[:override_timestamps] || @status.within_realtime_window?

97:       following_collection: @account.following_url,

100:     )

102:     attachment_ids = process_attachments.take(Status::MEDIA_ATTACHMENTS_LIMIT).map(&:id)

104:     @params = {

123:     }
124:   end

128:     # know here, because silent mentions would only be used for local access control anyway

18:       RemovalWorker.perform_async(status.id, { 'redraft' => false })
19:       num_deleted += 1

21:     end

todos.content: Step 2: Map the Status contract and shared pieces
Step 3: Trace status creation and distribution
Step 4: Audit every dependent of Status contract

content.text:
## Step 1: Orient in the codebase

This is the Mastodon server repository: a Ruby on Rails backend (`app/`), React/Redux/TypeScript frontend (`app/javascript/`), PostgreSQL, Redis, and Sidekiq. The backend is organized into:

- `app/models/` – ActiveRecord models and concerns.
- `app/services/` – synchronous business operations.
- `app/workers/` – Sidekiq background jobs.
- `app/lib/` – utilities, ActivityPub processing, and feed logic.
- `app/controllers/` – REST API, ActivityPub federation endpoints, and admin controllers.
- `app/serializers/` and `app/presenters/` – outbound representations.
- `app/policies/` – Pundit authorization.

The central content model is `Status` at `app/models/status.rb:34`. Feeds, notifications, federation, moderation, backup, and account lifecycle all revolve around it. It is built on shared concerns in `app/models/concerns/status/` and generic concerns in `app/models/concerns/`.

## Step 2: Map the Status contract and shared pieces

**Model:** `app/models/status.rb:34`

**Shared concerns included by `Status`:**
- `Cacheable` – cached association loading and cache-key versioning.
- `Discard::Model` (`self.discard_column = :deleted_at`) – soft-delete semantics.
- `Paginable` – pagination helpers.
- `RateLimitable` (`rate_limit by: :account, family: :statuses`).
- `Status::FaspConcern` – FASP lifecycle announcements.
- `Status::FetchRepliesConcern` – remote reply fetching scopes and eligibility.
- `Status::SafeReblogInsert` – DB-level guard against reblogs of discarded statuses.
- `Status::SearchConcern` – private search indexing and searchable text.
- `Status::SnapshotConcern` – edit snapshot support.
- `Status::ThreadingConcern` – ancestor/descendant recursive queries and filtering.
- `Status::Visibility` – visibility enum, scopes, and `distributable?`.
- `Status::InteractionPolicyConcern` – quote approval policy.

**Key associations exposed by the contract:**
- `belongs_to :account`, `belongs_to :thread`/`reblog` (self-referential), `belongs_to :preloadable_poll`.
- `has_one :status_stat`, `has_one :poll`, `has_one :preview_cards_status`, `has_one :trend`, `has_one :quote`, `has_one :notification` (polymorphic).
- `has_many :favourites`, `:bookmarks`, `:reblogs`, `:replies`, `:mentions`, `:media_attachments`, `:tagged_objects`, `:quotes`.

**Key callbacks and hooks:**
- `default_scope { recent.kept }` (`app/models/status.rb:128`).
- `after_create_commit :trigger_create_webhooks`, `:increment_counter_caches`, `:store_uri`, `:update_statistics`.
- `before_destroy :unlink_from_conversations!` (`app/models/status.rb:178`).
- `around_create Mastodon::Snowflake::Callbacks`.

## Step 3: Trace how a status is created and distributed

**Local post flow:**
1. `Api::V1::StatusesController#create` (`app/controllers/api/v1/statuses_controller.rb`) authorizes and calls `PostStatusService`.
2. `PostStatusService#call` (`app/services/post_status_service.rb:42`):
   - `process_status!` builds `@account.statuses.new(...)` and saves (`:98`).
   - `postprocess_status!` (`:162`) enqueues:
     - `LinkCrawlWorker.perform_async(@status.id)` (`:165`)
     - `DistributionWorker.perform_async(@status.id)` (`:166`)
     - `ActivityPub::DistributionWorker.perform_async(@status.id)` (`:168`)
     - `PollExpirationNotifyWorker` / `ActivityPub::QuoteRequestWorker` as needed.
3. `DistributionWorker` (`app/workers/distribution_worker.rb:10`) calls `FanOutOnWriteService`.
4. `FanOutOnWriteService#call` (`app/services/fan_out_on_write_service.rb:12`):
   - Pushes to author’s home feed.
   - Schedules `FeedInsertWorker` for followers, list members, and hashtag followers.
   - Schedules `LocalNotificationWorker` for mentions, quote targets, and update notifications.
   - Broadcasts public/unlisted statuses to Redis streaming timelines (`broadcast_to_public_streams!`, `broadcast_to_hashtag_streams!`).
5. `FeedInsertWorker` (`app/workers/feed_insert_worker.rb:10`) loads the status, applies `FeedManager` filters, inserts into home/list/tag feeds, and may call `LocalNotificationWorker`.
6. `ActivityPub::DistributionWorker` (`app/workers/activitypub/distribution_worker.rb:12`) uses `StatusReachFinder` and serializes the status for remote delivery.

**Remote (federation) creation flow:**
1. `ActivityPub::Activity::Create#perform` (`app/lib/activitypub/activity/create.rb:7`).
2. `create_status` (`:17`) validates, then `process_status` (`:45`) calls `Status.create!` (`:63`).
3. `distribute` (`:79`) enqueues `LinkCrawlWorker` and `DistributionWorker`.

**Update flow:**
- `Api::V1::StatusesController#update` → `UpdateStatusService` (`app/services/update_status_service.rb:19`), which snapshots edits, updates media/poll/text, and enqueues `DistributionWorker` (update mode) and `ActivityPub::StatusUpdateDistributionWorker`.

## Step 5: Trace how status identity and visibility are guarded

**Existence / identity checks:**
- `Status.find(status_id)` is the most common identity check; workers rescue `ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound` (`app/workers/distribution_worker.rb:12`, `app/workers/feed_insert_worker.rb:25`, `app/workers/activitypub/distribution_worker.rb:16`, etc.).
- `Status.with_discarded.find(status_id)` in `RemovalWorker` (`app/workers/removal_worker.rb:7`) allows removing already-soft-deleted statuses.
- `StatusFinder` (`app/lib/status_finder.rb:17`) resolves local URLs to `Status.find(...)`.
- `ResolveURLService` (`app/services/resolve_url_service.rb:100-110`) resolves by URI or local ID.

**Visibility / access guards:**
- `StatusPolicy#show?` (`app/policies/status_policy.rb:4`) – authorizes viewing based on author availability, direct/private visibility, mention/followership, and blocks.
- `Status::Visibility#distributable?` (`app/models/concerns/status/visibility.rb:31`) – `public? || unlisted?`; used to decide fan-out, streaming, and federation reach.
- `Status#hidden?` (`app/models/concerns/status/visibility.rb:27`) – inverse of `distributable?`.
- `StatusFilter#filtered?` (`app/lib/status_filter.rb:11`) – decides if a status should be hidden from a viewer based on blocks/mutes/policies.
- `AccountStatusesFilter#results` (`app/lib/account_statuses_filter.rb:20`) – scopes an account’s statuses for anonymous/author/follower/blocked viewers.
- `StatusReachFinder#distributable?` (`app/lib/status_reach_finder.rb:96`) – limits ActivityPub delivery to public/unlisted/private/direct audiences.
- `FanOutOnWriteService#broadcastable?` (`app/services/fan_out_on_write_service.rb:188`) – only public non-reblog statuses from non-silenced accounts go to public streams.
- `FeedManager` filter methods decide whether a status may enter a home/list/mentions feed.
- `Status.default_scope { recent.kept }` (`app/models/status.rb:128`) hides discarded statuses unless explicitly unscoped.

**Lifecycle / soft-delete guards:**
- `Discard::Model` provides `discarded?`, `kept?`, `with_discarded`, and `discard_with_reblogs`.
- `Status#discard_with_reblogs` (`app/models/status.rb:400`) soft-deletes a status and its reblogs.
- `RemoveStatusService` (`app/services/remove_status_service.rb:16`) decides `permanently?` (`:162`) based on `:immediate`, `:preserve`, and `reported?`.

## Step 6: Assess the blast radius of the contract change

**Highest risk (will break or silently misbehave if storage/visibility/teardown changes):**

1. **`app/models/status.rb` itself and its concerns** – the contract being changed.
2. **`app/services/fan_out_on_write_service.rb`** and **`app/lib/feed_manager.rb`** – any change to status identity, visibility, or counter caches directly affects what lands in timelines and how it is removed.
3. **`app/services/remove_status_service.rb`** and **`app/services/batched_remove_status_service.rb`** – teardown rework will hit these first; dependent associations, counter caches, and feed removal must stay consistent.
4. **`app/services/post_status_service.rb`** and **`app/services/update_status_service.rb`** – creation/update paths assume specific callbacks, scopes, and association behavior.
5. **`app/lib/activitypub/activity/create.rb`** and **`app/lib/activitypub/activity/delete.rb`** – federation ingress/egress depends on exact status identity, URI storage, and visibility.
6. **`app/workers/activitypub/distribution_worker.rb`**, **`StatusReachFinder`**, and AP delivery workers – changes to visibility or reach will propagate to other instances.
7. **`app/services/delete_account_service.rb`** – account deletion bypasses normal callbacks with `BatchedRemoveStatusService`; must still clean up statuses and stats.
8. **`app/services/backup_service.rb`** – backup archives assume statuses are enumerable, serializable, and that favourites/bookmarks resolve.

**High risk:**
- `app/services/notify_service.rb`, `LocalNotificationWorker`, `AccountConversation` – direct/private mention logic depends on `Status#direct_visibility?`, mentions, and conversation linking.
- `app/models/concerns/status/threading_concern.rb` – recursive CTEs assume the `statuses` table shape and soft-delete semantics.
- `app/models/concerns/status/snapshot_concern.rb` and `StatusEdit` – edit history depends on status content and media ordering.
- `app/models/media_attachment.rb` – media cleanup checks status references; changing status teardown could orphan media or delete it prematurely.
- `app/models/poll.rb`, `PreviewCardsStatus`, `Quote`, `StatusStat`, `StatusTrend` – these hold status foreign keys and depend on destroy/nullify behavior.
- `app/lib/vacuum/statuses_vacuum.rb`, `AccountStatusesCleanupPolicy`, `AccountStatusesCleanupService` – automated deletion policies rely on scopes and `discard_with_reblogs`.
- `app/chewy/statuses_index.rb`, `app/chewy/public_statuses_index.rb`, index importers – search indexing depends on `searchable_text`, `searchable_properties`, and scope `indexable`.

**Medium risk:**
- Controllers and serializers mostly consume the model; they will need updates if public methods (`distributable?`, `ordered_media_attachments`, counter methods) change signature.
- `app/presenters/status_relationships_presenter.rb` – depends on `Status.reblogs_map/favourites_map/etc.`
- `app/lib/mastodon/cli/*.rb` maintenance scripts – may need updates if bulk deletion logic changes.
